For admissions readers

Ian Brogan — Prospective Student

I grew up fascinated by the complex spiderwebs that create businesses — and equally drawn to the patience and craft of making things with your hands.

ianbrogan2@hotmail.com · @ianbrogan1227 · Download résumé PDF

My story

I've been teaching myself markets since I was ten — reading financial statements, building a personal investment account, learning how funds work. Not because anyone told me to. Because it felt like finding a language I already spoke.

A medically necessary year of independent study became the best thing that happened to me. It gave me time to learn 3D design, printing, woodworking, and graphic production. I built a resin table with my hands. I designed and vinyl-wrapped a custom 3D-printed spoiler shelf. I taught myself the full Adobe suite. I found out that I work best when I get to move between the precise and the tactile.

I was promoted to Lead Studio Director for my school's TV & Film program. I was entrusted with roughly $100,000 to revamp a CTE program from the ground up. I teach advanced cooking because I find the same satisfaction in a properly reduced sauce that I find in a properly constructed financial model.

My ADHD is my greatest asset. It's the engine behind the range. The thing that lets me care deeply about options pricing at 9 AM and be elbow-deep in resin work by 2 PM.

Where I'm applying

  • Cal Poly SLO Business Economics + Data Analytics Strong applied-learning culture. Practical foundation.
  • UC Davis Business Economics + potential Viticulture & Enology minor The vineyard thread runs directly through UC Davis.
  • UC Berkeley Business / Haas (if eligible) + Data Analytics The academic ceiling. Haas feeds directly into finance.

Experience

Lead Studio Director TV, Film & Digital Media Program · 2025–2026
  • Promoted to lead director overseeing broadcast-quality productions and the full digital media curriculum
  • Directed, edited, and color-graded content using Premiere Pro, After Effects, and the full Adobe suite
  • Mentored peers in production workflow, audio, and post-production
CTE Program Revamp — Lead Career Technical Education · Summer 2026
  • Entrusted with roughly $100,000 budget to overhaul and relaunch a CTE program from the ground up
  • Responsible for curriculum design, equipment procurement, and brand identity
Self-Directed Investor Personal · 2019–present
  • Built and managed a personal investment account from age ten — real capital, real decisions
  • Self-taught through primary sources: financial statements, earnings calls, fund letters
  • Focus on fundamental analysis, long-term compounding, and understanding how value is created

Skills

Finance & Data: Investment analysis, financial statements, Excel modeling, data analytics

Design & Media: Premiere Pro, After Effects, Photoshop, Lightroom, Illustrator, color grading, logo design, audio

Making & Craft: 3D design & printing, woodworking, resin casting, vinyl fabrication, crochet

Culinary: Advanced pastry, French technique, food styling & photography

Career vision

  1. Near term College Business Economics major. Data Analytics minor. Possibly Viticulture & Enology or Film/Production as a second minor.
  2. Early career BlackRock internship Family connection provides the foot in the door. I bring the prep work — years of self-directed market study.
  3. Building Junior → Senior data analyst Learn how institutional capital actually moves. Build the quantitative depth the fund management role demands.
  4. The goal Fund manager Not just managing returns — mastering the discipline of capital allocation at scale.
  5. The endgame Retire to the vineyard Where the two worlds finally merge. Precision in the growing season. Patience in the aging barrel. Craft at the end of a long, compounding arc.

Full portfolio and writing at https://ianbrogan.com
